title: Epic: Claude CLI Harness Web App
description: A lean Go web app that launches Claude CLI (--output-format=stream-json --verbose) in a goroutine, streams output via WebSocket to an HTML5 frontend, and renders conversation in a clean collapsible UI. Single instance only. Static binary with embedded assets.
close_reason: All stories complete - web app builds to 8MB static binary

title: Go project scaffolding with embedded static assets
description: Set up go.mod, main.go, and directory structure. Use Go embed to serve HTML/CSS/JS from a single binary. Pico CSS embedded (not CDN). HTTP server on localhost:8080 always. Directories: cmd/harness/, internal/, web/ (for static files). Build with CGO_ENABLED=0 for static linking. All state is ephemeral - nothing persists on close.
close_reason: Go project with embedded web assets, localhost:8080, static binary

title: Frontend: Tool use display with indented results
description: Tool calls (Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, etc.) rendered as a distinct block showing tool name + key inputs. Tool results indented beneath the tool call. Color code: green for success, red for errors. Show stdout/stderr from tool_use_result. Syntax highlighting for code blocks if feasible with vanilla JS.
close_reason: Tool use with indented results, color coding

title: Frontend: Question interaction - click to answer
description: When Claude's turn ends with a question (text ending in '?' or AskUserQuestion), render it as a clickable element. Clicking reveals a text input inline. User types answer and submits. The answer gets appended to the context file on disk, then a new claude invocation is launched with the updated context piped via stdin. Stream the new response back through the same WebSocket.
close_reason: Question detection on result ending with ?, inline answer input, submits as new prompt

title: Frontend: Context tab showing ~/.claude and config files
description: Tab in the UI called 'Context'. Shows: 1) Contents of ~/.claude/ directory listing, 2) Contents of ~/claude.json* files. Fetched via a REST endpoint on the Go backend. Read-only display. Clear button wipes the conversation context file (starting a fresh conversation) AND resets the output display.
close_reason: Context tab read-only with clear button

title: Keyboard shortcut 's' toggles auto-scroll
description: Press 's' key to toggle auto-scroll on/off. Only active when no input field is focused. When on: output area auto-scrolls to bottom as new events stream in. When off: stay where the user scrolled. Show a small indicator for current auto-scroll state. Default: auto-scroll on.
close_reason: s key toggles auto-scroll outside inputs

title: Frontend: Collapsible conversation groups (2-line default)
description: A turn is rendered live: every block (thinking, text, tool_use, tool_result) appears as a new line in real-time as events arrive. Tool results are indented as child lines under their parent tool_use. All top-level blocks are flush left, only tool results are indented. While the turn is active, everything is expanded and visible. When the turn completes, it collapses into a 2-line summary: line 1 = the user input that triggered this turn, line 2 = the final text response from the model. Click to expand, click to collapse.
close_reason: Collapsible turns with live rendering, 2-line summary

title: Claude CLI launcher goroutine with single-instance guard
description: Goroutine that spawns claude CLI in cwd. Context is a plain text conversation log (temp file, created at startup, deleted on exit). Invocation: 'cat context.txt | claude --output-format stream-json --verbose --print'. Single instance guard - reject while running, re-enable input when process ends. If claude crashes/times out, recover gracefully. Context file persists until user clicks Clear (which wipes the file, starting fresh). Build with CGO_ENABLED=0.
close_reason: CLI launcher with single-instance guard, demo mode, context file stdin

title: Frontend: Cost and usage display from result events
description: When a result event arrives (type=result), display session summary: total cost, duration, token usage (input/output/cache), model used. Show in a subtle footer or sidebar element.
close_reason: Cost/usage in turn summary and status bar

title: Frontend: HTML layout with input field and output area
description: Single-page HTML5 app. Top: text input field + submit button. Rest of page: scrollable output area. Minimal CSS - consider Pico CSS or similar classless framework for clean defaults. Dark theme friendly. No JS frameworks.
close_reason: HTML5 + Pico CSS dark theme, input + output layout

title: Stop button to kill running claude process
description: A visible Stop/Kill button in the main UI that sends SIGTERM to the running claude process. Available whenever a claude invocation is active. After kill: show whatever output was captured, re-enable the input field. POST /api/stop endpoint on the backend.

title: Stream JSON parser for Claude CLI output
description: Parse newline-delimited JSON from Claude CLI --output-format stream-json --verbose. Define Go structs for each event type: system/init, assistant (content types: text, thinking, tool_use), user (tool_result), rate_limit_event, result. For the context file: build a lean structured text transcript. Include user prompts and assistant final text responses. For tool calls: just tool name + key input (e.g. 'Tool(Bash): br ready'). For tool results: first 3 lines only, truncated with '...'. Skip thinking blocks and metadata from context. Goal: smallest possible context that preserves conversation continuity.
close_reason: Stream JSON parser with all event types, lean context builder

title: SSE + REST endpoints for real-time streaming
description: Use Server-Sent Events (SSE) for server-to-client streaming (GET /events) - zero dependencies, fully stdlib. Use regular POST /api/prompt for client-to-server (submit prompts, answer questions). POST /api/clear to wipe context. GET /api/context for the Context tab data. All stdlib net/http, no external WebSocket library.
